title: Sweet Potato Curry
description: A hearty and flavorful curry with sweet potatoes, quinoa, and lentils in a coconut milk base.
ingredients: 1 (15 ounce) can of beans (black, pinto, etc) [optional]
instructions: Add all ingredients to a large pot and stir until combined. Bring mixture to a boil, reduce to a simmer and simmer covered until liquid has absorbed and quinoa, lentils and sweet potato are cooked through, 50-60 minutes.

You will want to uncover the pot and give it a good stir every so often to make sure nothing is sticking to the bottom and that there is enough liquid. If for some reason you run out of liquid, you can add in a little bit more broth, or water.
Garnish with chopped cashews, fresh cilantro, or pickled red onions, if desired.
Add-ons: If you want to make it more hearty, you can add a can of beans (black, pinto, etc), some tempeh cut into bite sized pieces, TVP, or pea crumbles.

title: Harissa Chickpeas
description: A flavorful and spicy North African dish with chickpeas in a rich harissa sauce.
ingredients: 1 medium onion, thinly sliced, or about 1 1/2 cups
1/2 teaspoon salt, or more to taste, divided
1 tablespoon garlic paste, or 4 cloves of garlic, minced
2 cups of chopped leafy greens of choice, such as Swiss chard, spinach, mustard greens, etc
large can of chickpeas, drained or other white beans
1 cup full fat coconut milk or coconut cream
lemon juice, cilantro, and black pepper, for garnish
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Add the oil, and once it's hot, add the onion and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Mix well to coat, and continue to cook, stirring occasionally. If the pan is drying out too much, add 2 to 3 teaspoons of water to help the pan conduct heat and for the onions to brown evenly, about 7 to 9 minutes. Then once the onions are translucent and starting to get golden on some outer edges, add the balsamic vinegar and mix well. Continue to cook for another 3 to 4 minutes.

Then add in the garlic, smoked paprika, harissa, and oregano, and mix really well. Cook for a minute, so that the garlic gets cooked. Then add in the chard, remaining salt, the chickpeas, and the coconut cream and mix really well. Partially cover the pan, and simmer for 8 to 10 minutes, then taste and adjust the flavor. If you want more sauce, you can add more nondairy milk. If you used full fat coconut milk, the sauce probably didn't thicken as much, and if you want it to thicken even more, add in a tablespoon or two of non-dairy yogurt or sour cream. Add in a good squeeze of lemon juice and adjust salt and heat as needed.

Let everything simmer for another minute or two, if you want the sauce to thicken and glaze over the chickpeas. Switch off the heat and garnish with cilantro or parsley along with some black pepper and some more lemon juice. Serve with flatbread or rice or with some sourdough toast or garlic bread or whatever else that you like.

title: Quick Pickled Red Onions
description: Simple and tangy pickled red onions perfect for sandwiches and salads.
ingredients: .67 tablespoons salt per cup of pickling liquid
instructions: Cut the red onion and transfer into a heat safe jar with a lid. I typically make half moons with it, and cut them in half so they're shorter.
Mix your pickling liquid, add in any desired flavor customizations (peppercorns, etc), pour it into a small saucepan and bring to a boil.
Pour the pickling liquid into the jar, filling it up and ensuring the onion is submerged. You may want to press it down from the opening with a measuring spoon to ensure all the onion is submerged.

Let cool on the counter, and store in the fridge afterwards. You can use them pretty soon afterwards, once they're more pink than red (~15 mins), but waiting an hour or more may help the flavor develop more.

title: Kung Pao Chickpeas
description: A flavorful Asian-inspired dish with chickpeas in a savory Kung Pao sauce.
ingredients: 1 block tempeh, cut into small pieces (optional)
1 zucchini, sliced into bite sized pieces (optional)
3 cups cooked chickpeas (or two 15 oz. cans, rinsed and drained)
2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar (the more syrupy, the better)
.5 teaspoon garlic powder (or 2 minced garlic cloves)
.5 teaspoon ground ginger (or 2 teaspoons fresh minced ginger)
cooked white rice, for serving (or cauliflower rice)
a handful of cashews, as optional garnish
instructions: Add onion, tempeh (if using), and chickpeas into bowl of your slow cooker.
In a small bowl, whisk together tamari, vinegar, maple syrup, garlic, ginger, red pepper flakes, and sesame oil. Pour sauce into slow cooker and stir well.

Cover slow cooker and set it to cook for 3 hours on high heat, or 6 hours on low heat. If you need to cook it longer due to your schedule, I'd recommend adding 2-4 tablespoons of water to mixture to help prevent sauce from over-cooking.
30 - 60 mins before ready to eat, add in the bell pepper and zucchini (if using), so they hold their texture.
Once cooking is done, give chickpeas a stir and serve them warm over a bed of white rice (or cauliflower rice) with a sprinkling of green onions and sesame seeds on top.
Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the fridge for up to a week.
notes: Lots of veggies work well in this. If they're hearty, you can toss them in at the start, or if they're more delicate, add them closer to the end. Doubling the sauce can be a good idea. You can bulk it up with some tempeh, tvp/protein crumble, or nuts.

title: Creamy Coconut Curry Lentils with Spinach
description: Rich, creamy, and earthy coconut curry lentils that are an easy and delicious vegan option for dinner or weekly meal prep.
instructions: Mince the garlic, grate the ginger, and dice the onion. Add the olive oil, garlic, and ginger to a deep skillet, Dutch oven, or soup pot. Sauté the garlic and ginger over medium heat for 1 minute, or just until the garlic becomes soft and fragrant.
Add the diced onion to the skillet and continue to sauté over medium until the onion is soft and translucent. Add the curry powder and continue to sauté for about one minute more to toast the spices.

Add the dry lentils and vegetable broth to the skillet. Stir to dissolve any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet. Place a lid on top, turn the heat up to medium-high, and bring the broth to a boil. Once boiling, turn the heat down to low, and let it simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.

After simmering for 20 minutes the lentils should be tender and most of the broth absorbed. Add the can of coconut milk and stir to combine. Turn the heat back up to medium and allow the skillet to come back up to a simmer. Let it simmer without a lid for an additional 10 minutes, stirring often, to thicken the mixture.
Once thickened, turn the heat off. Add the fresh spinach and stir gently until the spinach has wilted. Taste the mixture and adjust the salt or curry powder to your liking, if needed.
Serve over a bowl of rice, and top with chopped cilantro if desired.
notes: If adding cashews, you can toss unroasted ones in along with the coconut milk. If already roasted, add them at the very end to avoid bitterness.

title: Spicy Gochujang Tomato Tofu
description: A spicy, umami and slightly sweet sauce coats chewy tofu pieces, cooked to golden perfection. This dish is super flavourful and made with only 7 ingredients, perfect as a quick weeknight meal or a lazy weekend dinner.

items: 11 oz firm tofu, pressed, cut into 1 cm cubes
1/4 cup gochujang, or less for less heat

instructions: Cook the rice according to the package instructions.
Meanwhile, heat the oil in a sauté pan over medium-high heat and cook the tofu until golden on all sides, about 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, mix the gochujang, garlic, soy sauce, and sugar in a bowl. Set aside.
When the tofu is golden, transfer it to a plate. Add the tomatoes to the now-empty pan, and cook for 3 minutes.
Add the sauce and greens, if using, and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
Stir in the tofu, then serve alongside the warm rice. Garnish as desired, and enjoy!
notes: Hearty greens work well, such as spinach or kale. If using kale, add a little earlier in the process.

title: The Best Vegan Apple Crisp
description: I know what you're thinking. The best apple crisp, really? Friends, I know this is a big claim, but I've had a lot of apple crisps in my life and know this is a one.
ingredients: 1/2 cup melted coconut oil or olive oil
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (176°C).
Peel apples, quarter, remove cores, and use a paring knife to thinly slice lengthwise.
Add to a large mixing bowl and top with remaining filling ingredients. Toss to combine.
Add to a 9×13-inch baking dish and spread evenly.
Rinse and wipe out your mixing bowl and add all topping ingredients. Stir to combine, then use fingers to break down any clumps of sugar. Pour over apples in an even layer.
Bake for 50 minutes to 1 hour (uncovered) or until filling is bubbly, apples are very fork tender, and topping is deep golden brown.
Let rest at least 30 minutes before serving. This is delicious with coconut whipped cream or vanilla bean coconut ice cream.
Best when fresh, though leftovers keep covered in refrigerator up to 3 days, or in freezer up to 1 month. Reheat in microwave or in a 350°F (176°C) oven until warmed through.
notes: Consider swapping some water for hard cider, or add a splash of bourbon/whiskey to add more depth. Adding some sour from rhubarb or cranberries levels things up.

title: Crispy 5-Minute Seitan
description: A quick and easy way to get in a healthy dose of plant-based protein, especially if you don't have a gluten sensitivity. This savory version is incredibly versatile.
instructions: Preheat your waffle maker or panini press and spray with oil.
In a medium bowl, combine all dry ingredients: vital wheat gluten, nutritional yeast, PB powder, garlic powder, onion powder, and salt.
Add water and stir until a dough forms. Knead it lightly for 30 seconds to activate the gluten.
Place the dough into your preheated waffle maker and cook for 5 minutes, or until golden and firm.
Let cool slightly, then break apart into chunks.
notes: Powdered peanut butter is not necessary. A splash of soy sauce, or spicy heat works well.

title: Protein Baked Oatmeal
description: Have your oatmeal and get your protein in with this protein baked oatmeal! It's easy to make, perfect for meal prep and packs over 17 grams of protein per serving.
ingredients: 2 flax eggs (2 tbsp ground flax + 5 tbsp water)
1/4 cup fresh blueberries, plus more for topping
nut butter, for drizzling on top
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F and spray a 8-inch square baking dish with cooking spray.
In a large bowl, mix together the oats, protein powder, milk, peanut butter, eggs, maple syrup, coconut oil, vanilla and salt.
Stir in blueberries and pour oatmeal mixture into the prepared baking dish. Top with additional blueberries.
Bake for 35-40 minutes or until center is set. Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes. Portion and serve with a drizzle of nut butter.
notes: If using sweetened milk, reduce maple syrup to preference.

title: Vegan Chicken Fajitas
description: This easy vegan chicken fajitas recipe is so flavorful and easy to make! Great for the grill too! Perfect for meal prep all week!
ingredients: cilantro and lime or lemon juice for garnish
instructions: Mix all the spices (from seasoned salt to red pepper flakes) in a small bowl, or grab taco seasoning
In a large bowl, add the dehydrated soy curls and pour the boiling stock carefully over. Stir together. Let soak for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, to make sure all are coated well.
Set the oven to 400°F. Remove excess liquid from soy curls, prep veggies and add 1-2 Tbps of seasoning and 1 Tbps of oil to each.
Add vegetables and soy curls to a large baking tray and bake for 20 minutes. Line the veggies so they don't burn or stick. Soy curls will crisp better directly on sheet.
Remove from oven and serve in shells or lettuce wraps with favorite toppings.

title: Sheet Pan Teriyaki Tofu and Broccoli
description: Sheet pan teriyaki tofu and broccoli is simple yet full of flavor and perfect to add to the weekly menu rotation! You'll love how quick it is!
ingredients: 1 block extra firm tofu, pressed and cubed
instructions: In a large bowl, add cubed tofu, cornstarch, soy sauce, and oil. Stir to combine well.
In the same bowl, add broccoli and sugar snap peas. Drizzle with oil and add salt and pepper to taste. Stir together well, then add to same tray with tofu.
Arrange into a single layer on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake at 425°F for 18-20 minutes until tofu has golden brown edges and broccoli is bright green and tender.
While baking, make the teriyaki sauce by whisking together soy sauce, water, brown sugar, rice vinegar, ginger, garlic, and pepper flakes in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil.
Add cornstarch and water mixture to the boiling sauce and stir until thickened, about 1-2 minutes.
Remove tofu and veggies from oven, add to a large bowl and toss with the teriyaki sauce.
Serve over rice, quinoa, or noodles and enjoy!

title: Sweet Potato Black Bean Enchiladas
description: Hearty and flavorful enchiladas with roasted sweet potatoes, black beans, and kale in a smoky red enchilada sauce. A plant-based twist on a classic Mexican dish.
ingredients: 1 tbsp coconut or avocado oil (or sub water)
2 cups chopped kale (or other sturdy green)
1 15-ounce can black beans (drained, or sub pinto or refried beans)
3 cups Red Enchilada Sauce (divided, or store-bought)
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (204°C) and position a rack in the middle of oven.
Add cubed sweet potato to one large baking sheet and drizzle with oil, cumin, paprika, and salt. Toss to combine.
Bake for 20-25 minutes or until sweet potatoes are fork tender and slightly caramelized. Set aside to cool. Also reduce oven heat to 350°F (176°C).

In the meantime, add your kale to a large cast-iron or metal skillet over medium heat with 2 tbsp water. Cover and steam for about 4-5 minutes or until kale is slightly softened but still vibrant green. Uncover and set aside.
Add drained black beans to a mixing bowl with steamed kale and roasted sweet potatoes. Add smaller measurement of enchilada sauce to kale and sweet potatoes and stir to combine.
Wrap tortillas in damp cloth towel and microwave to warm for 30 seconds to make more pliable. Alternatively, place wrapped tortillas directly on oven rack for a few minutes to heat through.
Pour about one third of enchilada sauce into the bottom of a 9×13-inch baking dish. Spread to coat.

Lay one corn tortilla down in the saucy dish to coat. Then flip it over to coat the other side. Fill with ~1/3 cup of filling. Then roll up and lay seam-side down at the edge of the dish. Continue until all tortillas are filled and rolled, adding more sauce as needed to moisten tortillas.
Pour remaining enchilada sauce over the top of the enchiladas in a stripe down the middle. Use less sauce for slightly drier enchiladas.
Bake at 350°F (176°C) for 15-20 minutes or until warmed through. Top with desired toppings and serve.
notes: Perfect for meal prep! The sweet potatoes can be roasted ahead of time, and the filling comes together quickly. Serve with avocado crema, jalapeños, and fresh cilantro for a complete meal.

title: Gochujang Noodles
description: Spicy and savory Korean-inspired noodles with gochujang sauce, vegetables, and sesame seeds. A quick 30-minute flavor-packed dinner.

items: 2 tbsp soy sauce (use tamari for GF)
1 ½ tbsp organic brown sugar (see note 2)
1 bunch scallions, whites and light greens chopped
1 inch piece ginger, grated or minced
6 to 8 cups Napa cabbage, thinly sliced

instructions: Cook noodles according to package instructions, but cook for 1 minute less to prevent soggy dish. Drain, reserving some noodle water, and set aside.
Make the gochujang sauce by whisking the gochujang, toasted sesame oil, soy sauce, mirin, sugar, and gochugaru in a bowl.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add scallion whites and greens, garlic, and ginger, as well as vegetables like bell peppers or broccoli.
Sauté for a few minutes, then add delicate vegetables like Napa cabbage, bok choy leaves, or snow peas. Stir-fry until crisp-tender.
Pour the gochujang sauce into the pan along with the sesame seeds. Next, add the cooked noodles and toss to coat. If needed, add a splash of noodle water to help bring the sauce together.
Take off the heat and garnish with reserved scallion greens and Thai basil or cilantro. Gently toss, then enjoy!
notes: Perfect for quick weeknight dinners! The noodles stay chewy and the sauce clings perfectly. Add extra vegetables or use different noodle types as needed.

title: Hearty Vegan Chorizo Chili
description: A thick and hearty vegan chili packed with plant-based chorizo, black beans, salsa, and warming spices. Ready in 30 minutes with only 9 simple ingredients!
ingredients: ½ bunch cilantro, stems finely chopped and leaves divided
1 15-ounce (425g) can black beans, drained and rinsed
16 ounces chunky salsa (mild, medium, or hot)
instructions: Cook the chorizo, onion, garlic, and cilantro stems in a large nonstick pot over medium-high heat. Sauté for 3-5 minutes until onions are translucent. Add cumin and cook for 60 seconds.
Add bell pepper, beans, salsa, and diced tomatoes. Fill the salsa jar with water, give it a good shake, and add to the pot. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium-low and simmer.
Simmer for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally. Divide into serving bowls and top with cilantro leaves. Serve with tortilla chips. Leftovers keep in fridge for up to 5 days.
notes: Great for meal prep! The chorizo adds authentic flavor and beans make it hearty.
Can't find vegan chorizo? Use vegan ground beef with 1-2 tsp taco seasoning.
Alternative proteins: finely chopped mushrooms or cooked lentils work well.
Top with tortilla chips or serve over rice for a complete meal.
This chili freezes well for up to 2 months.

title: One-Pot Vegan Chili Mac
description: A protein-packed chili meets pasta in this one-pot wonder. Easy to make with simple ingredients and perfect for weeknight dinners.

items: 1 ½ tsp kosher salt, plus more to taste
1 can pinto, kidney, or black beans, drained and rinsed
1 can sweet corn, drained and rinsed
12 ounces elbow pasta or cavatappi pasta (gluten-free if needed)
2-3 tsp agave nectar, maple syrup, coconut sugar, or brown sugar

instructions: In a Dutch oven or large nonstick pot, heat 1 tbsp oil over medium heat. Once hot, add onions and cook for 2 minutes, seasoning with a pinch of salt.

Add garlic, mushrooms, and tempeh and cook for 5 more minutes. Add chili powder, paprika, oregano, cumin, cayenne pepper, and salt. Stir spices into vegetables and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Pour in vegetable broth to deglaze pan, scraping up any browned bits on the bottom of the pot.
Add in beans, crushed tomatoes, corn, and pasta. Stir well to combine.
Bring mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to maintain a simmer. Cook for 12-15 minutes, or until pasta is cooked through but not soggy.
If you want it to be a bit saucier, add a splash more of broth or water. Season to taste with salt and pepper, and stir in the agave or other sweetener.

If using vegan cheese, sprinkle it all over the top. Cover and let warm through for a few minutes. Allow chili mac to rest at least 15 minutes before serving and top with fresh chopped cilantro and any other desired toppings.

title: Smashed Edamame Toast
description: Protein-packed edamame smash with creamy tahini sauce and fresh herbs. A perfect 20-minute high-protein breakfast or snack.

items: 1 ½ tbsp soy sauce or tamari
1/2 to 1 serrano or chipotle pepper roughly chopped
1 big handful of cilantro leaves and tender stems remove thick stems
1 tsp toasted sesame oil more to taste

instructions: Add the tahini, edamame, avocado, soy sauce, garlic, chile pepper, cilantro, sesame oil, and lime zest and juice to a food processor. Start blending until it turns into a chunky dip. Season with salt to taste as needed.
Toast your bread of choice. Smear a generous amount of the edamame smash onto each slice of bread.
Top with hemp seeds and sauerkraut or pickled onions, if desired.
Store leftover spread in an airtight container in the fridge for 7 to 10 days. The green color will fade but it will still taste good.
notes: Great for high-protein breakfast! The edamame provides complete protein and the tahini adds healthy fats. This is perfect for meal prep - make a batch and enjoy throughout the week!

title: Tofu Noodle Bowl with Almond Butter Sauce
description: A protein-packed noodle bowl with crispy tofu, broccoli, and a creamy almond butter sauce. Ready in 20 minutes with big flavors and 33g of protein per serving.
ingredients: 8 ounces (227g) extra firm tofu, pressed 10 minutes
4 ounces (113g) rice noodles or soba noodles
1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water
Sriracha or chili garlic sauce to taste
instructions: Press tofu for 10 minutes to remove excess water.
While tofu presses, prepare the sauce by whisking together almond butter, vegetable broth, soy sauce, lime juice, maple syrup, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger until smooth.
Cook broccoli and carrots for 3-4 minutes until bright green and crisp-tender. Add green onions and cook for 2 minutes more.
Add the sauce and tofu to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes, until everything is heated through.
Add cooked noodles and toss to combine. If the sauce seems too thick, add a splash of vegetable broth or water.
Serve warm, garnished with sesame seeds and sliced green onions.
notes: Excellent protein source! Tofu provides complete protein while the almond butter sauce adds healthy fats. The vegetables add fiber and nutrients. This recipe is great for meal prep - the flavors get even better overnight!

title: Garlicky Quinoa and Lentils with Ricotta
description: Hearty quinoa and lentils with a creamy ricotta sauce packed with vegetables and herbs. A nutritious and filling 30-minute meal.
ingredients: 1 medium lemon, zested, and 1 tbsp juice, plus more taste
1/2 to 1 tsp red pepper flakes (to taste)
Pickled onions or sauerkraut for topping (optional)
instructions: Make quinoa. Bring 1 ¼ cups of water (300 mL) to a boil in a medium saute pan. Once boiling, add a pinch of salt and quinoa. Reduce heat to low and cook, covered, until liquid has evaporated and quinoa is tender, about 12 minutes. Open pot and fluff with a fork. Rinse out pan if you'd like to save dishes and use again in step 3. Optional: For best texture, if you have time, transfer quinoa to a sheet pan and spread out in an even layer.

Make ricotta. Meanwhile, drain tofu and dab away some excess water with a dish towel. Crumble tofu into a food processor bowl. Add nutritional yeast, garlic and onion powder, salt and pepper, olive oil, and lemon zest and 1 tbsp lemon juice. Blend until creamy and smooth. Taste for seasonings, adding more lemon or salt as needed.

Make spiced garlicky pepitas. Return saute pan to medium heat and add olive oil. Once warm but not hot, add garlic. Shake pan often and use a spatula to separate slices, and cook for about 1 minute. Add coriander and pepitas, and cook for another 2 to 3 minutes. If garlic starts to brown, take pan off the heat for a bit or lower the heat. In the last 30 seconds, add red pepper flakes and a big pinch of flaky sea salt. Take off heat to prevent garlic from browning.
To assemble, add quinoa and lentils to a bowl. Add several dollops of ricotta. Pour spiced garlicky pepitas on top, then finish with pickled onions or sauerkraut.
notes: All the base components are some shade of beige, which is not the most appetizing. To add some color, top it with some pickled onions and parsley. This recipe uses pre-cooked lentils for convenience but you can cook your own if you have time. The combination of quinoa and lentils provides complete protein with all essential amino acids!

title: Buffalo Chickpea Salad
description: Made with just 6 ingredients and flavorful hot sauce, this Buffalo Chickpea Salad is a quick and delicious base for a hearty snack or meal!
instructions: In a mixing bowl, add the hot sauce, tahini, and garlic and mix together.
Add the drained and rinsed chickpeas, and mash until chunky with desired texture.
Add the diced celery and onion, and stir to combine.
Serve with crackers, on salad or as a sandwich!
Store this buffalo chickpea salad in an airtight container in the fridge for about 4-5 days.
notes: This salad is perfect for meal prep and can be served in multiple ways - as a sandwich filling, with crackers, or on top of a green salad. The flavor gets even better after sitting in the fridge for a day!

title: Lentil Unstuffed Shells with Whipped Lemon Ricotta
description: Skip the stuffing hassle and focus on a ricotta so good no one misses the dairy. This deconstructed stuffed shells recipe features creamy lemon tofu ricotta and a hearty lentil tomato sauce.

items: 14 ounces firm tofu, drained and pressed
14 ounces canned brown lentils, drained and rinsed

instructions: Make the whipped lemon ricotta: In a blender, add drained tofu, lemon zest, lemon juice, olive oil, nutritional yeast, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. Blend until light, creamy, and whipped. Taste and adjust lemon or salt. Chill in fridge until ready to serve.
Prepare the spice blend: In a small bowl, combine dried oregano, garlic powder, onion powder, dried basil, dried parsley, Italian seasoning, dried thyme, salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes.

Cook the pasta: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook shells according to package instructions until just al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water before draining. Rinse lightly with cool water to stop cooking and set aside with a drizzle of oil.

Make the breadcrumbs: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add breadcrumbs and stir 2-3 minutes until golden. Add minced garlic and Italian seasoning. Stir together and cook another 30-60 seconds. Turn off heat and spread on parchment-lined baking tray.

Start the lentil sauce: In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add diced onion and sauté 5-7 minutes until translucent. Stir in minced garlic, tomato paste, and the spice blend. Stir constantly for 1 minute.

Build the sauce: Add nutritional yeast, coconut sugar, and vegetable stock. Stir and scrape the bottom of the pan. Reduce heat to medium low. Add fire roasted tomatoes and tomato sauce. Simmer 8-10 minutes. Add baking soda to balance acidity if desired.
Complete the sauce: Stir in lentils, half the reserved pasta water, and soy sauce. Simmer 2-3 minutes until thickened slightly, using more pasta water if needed. Fold in fresh basil.

Assemble the dish: Add pasta to the sauce and stir well. Cook 2-3 minutes until heated through. Spoon whipped lemon ricotta into bowls, top with sauce and shells, and finish with breadcrumbs. Optionally garnish with more basil, chives, red pepper flakes, or black pepper.
notes: This deconstructed approach saves time while delivering all the flavors of traditional stuffed shells.
The whipped lemon ricotta can be made ahead and stored in the fridge for up to 3 days.
Leftovers keep well in the fridge for 4-5 days and the flavors actually improve overnight.
For extra protein, you can add additional lentils or some white beans to the sauce.
The breadcrumbs add great texture - don't skip them! They can be made gluten-free with GF breadcrumbs.

title: Sheet Pan Za'atar Chickpeas with Sweet Potatoes
description: Crispy chickpeas and tender vegetables with Middle Eastern spices on one sheet pan. A simple, flavorful vegan and gluten-free meal.

items: 1 large sweet potato, peeled and diced

instructions: Preheat the oven to 405°F and line or grease a large baking sheet.
Place the chickpeas on a clean kitchen towel or paper towel and gently rub to dry well. Discard any loose skins, then transfer the chickpeas to a medium bowl.
Add the za'atar, salt, and a generous drizzle of olive oil to the chickpeas and mix well. Set aside.
Add the sweet potato, bell pepper, and onion directly to the baking sheet. Sprinkle the cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder, and salt over the veggies, then drizzle with olive oil.
Toss the vegetables until evenly combined, then push them to one side of the baking sheet. Add the chickpeas to the open space and spread out evenly.
Bake on the top rack of the oven for 20 minutes, then flip the veggies and chickpeas.
Return to the oven and bake for an additional 15 minutes, until the chickpeas are crispy.
Transfer the chickpeas and vegetables to serving bowls and serve with rice or pita and tahini sauce, or as desired.
notes: Za'atar is a Middle Eastern spice blend; you can usually purchase it at grocery stores or make your own.
For extra crispy chickpeas, make sure they're very dry before seasoning and baking.

title: Smoky Red Lentil Stew with Chickpeas
description: This smoky Red Lentil Stew is pure comfort in a bowl, ready in about 30 minutes with a deep, smoky flavor that tastes like it simmered all day.
ingredients: 1 (15 oz) can chickpeas, drained and rinsed
For serving: lemon wedges and minced fresh parsley
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Once shimmering, add carrots, celery, onion, and a sprinkle of salt. Sauté for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are softened and lightly browned (lower heat if needed).
Add garlic and sauté for 30-60 seconds, until fragrant.
Now add tomato paste, thyme, and smoked paprika. Sauté for 30-60 seconds, until fragrant.

Add lentils and stir to coat. Now add tomatoes, chickpeas, broth, salt, and pepper. Raise heat to bring to a boil, then reduce heat to maintain a gentle simmer. Cover and cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until lentils are tender.
Stir in spinach and cook uncovered for about 1 minute, until wilted. Stir in remaining tbsp of olive oil. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve hot with lemon wedges, fresh-cracked pepper, and a sprinkle of parsley.
notes: For extra smokiness, add more smoked paprika or add a few dashes of liquid smoke near the end of cooking. Add extra brightness by stirring in 1-2 tbsp of lemon juice at the end of cooking. For a creamier texture, cook a little longer until the lentils break down more or blend a portion of the finished dish. You can also stir in 1/3 cup of cashew cream.

title: Crispy Orange Tofu
description: This Crispy Orange Tofu is drenched in a sweet & tangy citrus sauce for a cozy meal that's better than takeout! Vegan & Gluten-Free.

items: 1 (14 ounce) block extra firm tofu, pressed
1 tbsp low sodium tamari or soy sauce
3 cloves garlic, finely minced or grated
1 cup freshly squeezed orange juice, strained

instructions: Preheat the oven to 405°F and line a baking sheet with a silicone mat or parchment paper. Tear the tofu into bite-sized chunks for a more chicken-like texture, or cut into small cubes.

Add the tofu to a medium bowl and add in the oil and tamari. Toss with a spatula until evenly coated, then sprinkle the cornstarch on top and toss again. Transfer to a baking sheet and spread out the tofu pieces so they aren't touching. Bake on the top rack of the oven for 30 minutes, flipping the tofu halfway through.

Once the tofu has gone into the oven, begin prepping the sauce. Warm the sesame oil in a medium pan over medium heat. Once warm, add the garlic, ginger, and optional red chili flakes. Sauté for 60 seconds, then add in the orange juice, brown sugar, tamari, vinegar, and orange zest.

Bring to a simmer over medium heat and cook for 3 to 5 minutes. Mix the cornstarch with the water until dissolved, then add the slurry to the sauce and simmer for another 3 to 5 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and can coat the back of a spoon.
Remove the tofu from the oven and add it to the pan, coating all sides of the tofu in the glossy orange sauce. Serve warm, over rice or as desired.
notes: This recipe works great with the crispy tofu, but you can also make the orange sauce and coat it in store-bought vegan chicken or veg chicken nuggets! I recommend using fresh squeezed orange juice for this – pasteurized will work in a pinch, but it will be less sweet and more bitter! You'll need 3-4 large oranges to get ~1 cup of juice. Brown sugar can be replaced with maple syrup or coconut sugar. Cornstarch can be swapped with arrowroot powder to help thicken the sauce. If you plan on making this recipe for meal prep, I would keep the tofu and sauce separate; warm them separately, then toss right before serving. This will keep your tofu crispy!

title: Tahini Dressing
description: You only need 3 simple ingredients to make this amazing Tahini Dressing! It's customizable, perfect for Meal Prep, and delicious on just about everything.

items: 1/2 cup water, divided; plus more to taste
1-2 tbsp chopped fresh herbs like dill or parsley

instructions: If you are using fresh garlic, combine it with the lemon juice and let sit for 5 minutes for a more mellow flavor.
Add the tahini, lemon juice, salt, and any additional add-ins to a medium bowl. Whisk together; the tahini will become very thick and lighter in color.
Slowly pour 1/4 cup of the water into the bowl as you whisk it into the mixture. Slowly add the remaining water to the bowl while you mix the dressing vigorously.
If you would like your dressing to be thinner, continue to add water slowly and whisk until the dressing reaches your desired consistency.
Enjoy immediately or store in the fridge for up to one week. The dressing will thicken in the fridge, but you can thin it out with a bit of water if necessary.
notes: If your tahini is on the thicker side, you may need to add more water to thin the dressing out more.

title: Slow-Cooker Lentil Sloppy Joes
description: The classic sloppy joe has always been a favourite of kids and adults alike. This recipe combines an old standby with the nutrition of lentils and the convenience of cooking in your slow-cooker!
ingredients: 2 tsp (10 mL) tamari or soy sauce
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Stir in diced onion, red pepper, and carrot. Saute for 5 minutes, until vegetables are slightly softened.
Stir in garlic and spices. Cook for another 2 minutes. Remove skillet from heat.
Add cooked vegetables along with remaining ingredients and 1 1/2 cups (375 mL) water into a 4 or 5-quart slow cooker. Cook for 4 hours on high, or 8 hours on low, until lentils are tender.
Season to taste with salt and pepper. Serve on toasted buns and top with grated cheddar cheese.

title: Slow Cooker Black Bean & Mango Caribbean Chili
description: A savory and sweet taste of the tropics, this vegan Caribbean chili is sure to shake up your Crock Pot chili rotation. In a good way.
ingredients: 2 14.5-ounce cans fire-roasted diced tomatoes (plain diced tomatoes also work)
4 cups cooked black beans (or 2 15-ounce cans black beans, drained)
1/2 cup orange juice (fresh-squeezed from about 2 medium naval oranges)
2 cups diced ripe mango (from about 2 large mangoes)
instructions: Add all ingredients except the mangoes and additional salt and pepper to a 3-quart or larger Crock Pot. Stir gently to combine.
Cook on low for 8-10 hours (or on high for 4-6 hours).
Stir in the mango, cover, and cook for another 10 minutes or so - just enough to warm the mangoes.
Taste and add additional salt and pepper if desired.
Scoop into large bowls and top individual servings with an assortment of toppings, if you like.
notes: Topping ideas: sour cream or cashew sour cream, lime wedges, diced avocado, sliced scallions, chopped mango, halved cherry tomatoes, cilantro.

title: Vegan Pumpkin Pie
description: This is the best vegan pumpkin pie and the only recipe you'll ever need! It's rich, creamy, deliciously spiced and perfect for the holidays.
ingredients: 15 ounce Can Pumpkin Purée (425g) not pumpkin pie filling
¾ cup Coconut Milk (180ml) Canned, Full Fat, Unsweetened
instructions: Prepare the crust: Add all purpose flour, sugar and salt to the food processor and pulse to combine.
Add the coconut oil (must be solid, not melted) and pulse until the mixture is crumbly.
Add ice water and pulse it to combine. It should look like a dough now and if you break off a piece and roll it in your hands it should easily roll into a ball.
Remove the dough from the food processor, transfer to a floured surface and roll the dough into a big round ball.
Using a rolling pin roll out the dough from side to side into a large circle that is wider than your 9-inch pie dish.

Spray your pie dish with non-stick spray and then roll up the crust in your rolling pin (or just pick it up carefully) and carefully unroll it over the top of your pie dish. Carefully tuck the crust in along the edges of your pie dish.
Take a scissors and trim off any excess dough but make sure that there is a little overhang over the edges of the dish. Place your pie crust into the fridge while you prepare your pie filling.

Prepare your filling: Add canned pumpkin, coconut milk, brown sugar, maple syrup, cornstarch, pumpkin pie spice, vanilla extract and salt to your blender and blend until smooth. If you don't have a blender, then just add all ingredients to a mixing bowl and use a hand whisk to whisk it until smooth.
Pour this out over your pie crust and smooth down with the back of a spoon.
Place into the oven to bake for 60 minutes at 350°F. To prevent over-browning, bring it out after 40 minutes, tent it with foil and then return to the oven for the remaining 20 minutes.

When you remove the pie from the oven, it will still be quite wobbly in the center, this is completely fine, it will firm up when cooling. Your pie may also be quite dark in color when first removed from the oven, it lightens up a lot when cool.
Allow to cool on the counter and then place into the refrigerator to set completely, around 4 hours at least or overnight if possible until completely chilled and set. Serve with vegan whipped cream.
notes: Make sure to use pumpkin purée, not pumpkin pie filling. The coconut oil for the crust must be solid, not melted. The pie needs to chill for at least 4 hours to set completely.

title: Easy Oatmeal Pie Crust
description: Tired of graham cracker crusts? This 5 ingredient, brown sugar, easy oatmeal pie crust makes a unique base to creamy, no-bake pies.
ingredients: 1/3 cup dark or light brown sugar (74 grams)
1/3 cup cold unsalted butter (5 tablespoons plus 1 teaspoon)
instructions: Combine the oats, flour, sugar and salt.
Using a pastry cutter, cut in the butter until the mixture is crumbly.
Press it firmly on the bottom and sides of lightly greased 9″ pie plate.
Bake at 375°F for 13-15 minutes or until the crust is lightly browned.
If the crust has slid at all, as the crust cools slightly, gently push the crust back up the sides of the pie plate.
Once the crust has cooled completely, fill it with any desired cream filling.
notes: This crust is perfect for cream pies and no-bake pies. You can make it in advance and store it until ready to use.

title: Good Morning Breakfast Cookies
description: An easy vegan breakfast cookies recipe for yummy energy cookies! The best gluten free breakfast recipe!
ingredients: ¾ cup unsweetened shredded coconut, divided
2 flax eggs (2 tablespoons ground flax + 6 tablespoons water)
½ cup natural, unsalted almond butter
¾ cup pitted dates, chopped (about 6-8 medjool dates)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper or greased foil. Set aside.
In a large bowl, whisk together all of the dry ingredients: oats, flour, ½ cup coconut, cinnamon, baking powder, baking soda and salt.
In a medium bowl, whisk together all of the wet ingredients: flax eggs, coconut oil, almond butter, maple syrup, coconut sugar and vanilla. Whisk until smooth.

Pour the wet mixture over the dry mixture. Use a rubber spatula or wooden spoon to fold the ingredients together until you get a cohesive cookie dough. Fold in almonds, cashews, dates and remaining ¼ cup coconut.
Scoop about 3 tablespoons of cookie dough into your hands. Shape into a ball and place evenly apart on the prepared cookie sheet. Flatten cookies with a fork to keep the texture.
Bake for 8-11 minutes.
Using a spatula, carefully remove cookies from the baking sheet and place onto a cooling rack. Allow to cool, then enjoy!
notes: If using homemade oat flour, make sure to sift the flour to remove coarse bits of oats that could create crumbly cookies. Store in an airtight container for 1-2 weeks.

title: Breakfast Egg Muffins
description: These easy breakfast egg muffins are made in a muffin tin with eggs, cheese, and veggies - great for meal prepping and using fridge leftovers.
ingredients: ½ cup red bell peppers, diced
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line a 12-cup muffin pan with silicone cups or parchment paper cups.
Crack the eggs into a large bowl or measuring cup with a spout. Add the milk, oregano, salt, and pepper, and whisk until smooth. Set aside.
Add the spinach, peppers, onions, and cheese into the muffin cups. Pour the egg mixture on top until the liquid almost reaches the tops of the molds, about ⅓ cup per muffin cup.
Bake until the egg cups are puffed and the center is solid and no longer jiggly, about 18–22 minutes.
Cool in the pan for 5–10 minutes. Run a butter knife around the edges to help loosen them if needed. Serve immediately.
notes: Great for meal prep! Store in the refrigerator for up to 5 days. Reheat in the microwave for 30-60 seconds.

title: One Bowl Chocolate Chunk Chai Banana Muffins
description: The best and (easiest) One Bowl Chocolate Chunk Chai Banana Muffins …pantry staple ingredients, super quick to mix up, healthy…ish, and SO DELICIOUS!
ingredients: 4 medium overly ripe bananas, mashed (about 1 1/4 cup mashed)
1 1/2 cups whole wheat pastry flour, or all-purpose flour
1 cup semi-sweet or dark chocolate chunks
4 tablespoons salted butter, at room temperature
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line 12 muffin tins with paper liners.
In a large mixing bowl, stir together the mashed bananas, coconut oil, honey, eggs, and vanilla until combined.
Add the flour, baking soda, cinnamon, ginger, cardamom, all-spice, and salt, mix until just combined. Fold in the chocolate chunks.
Divide the batter among the prepared muffin cups. Transfer to the oven and bake for 20-22 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Meanwhile, make the honey butter. In a small bowl, combine the butter, honey, and salt.
Serve the muffins warm or at room temperature smeared with honey-butter.
notes: For a gluten-free version, use Cup4Cup gluten free flour. The muffins can be stored in an airtight container for up to 3 days.

title: Crispy Lentil Wraps
description: These high-protein Vegan Lentil Wraps are quick and easy to make with fresh veggies, brown lentils, hummus, and green sauce for a filling, flavor-packed lunch!
ingredients: 1-2 tablespoons green sauce (pesto, chimichurri, zhoug, etc)
1 cup of baby spinach, arugula, or mixed greens
1 small Persian cucumber, ends trimmed and thinly sliced (or other veg)
1-2 teaspoons olive oil, optional, for pan frying
instructions: Drain and rinse your lentils if using canned. Place them on a clean paper towel or kitchen towel and gently pat dry to remove excess water.
Add the lentils to a small bowl along with the hummus and green sauce. Gently mix well, until the lentils are evenly coated and will stick together.

Place the mixed greens in the center of your wrap, then spread the cucumber slices evenly over the top and press them down slightly. Use a spoon to place the lentils on top of the cucumber, forming a long log shape. Fold the shorter ends of your wrap in, then roll it up.

Warm the olive oil in a medium nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add the wrap, with the open folded side down, and place a heavy flat object on top of it (such as a smaller pot lid). Sear the wrap for 5 minutes, then use a spatula to flip and sear for an additional 2 to 3 minutes, until golden.
Slice in half and serve with hot sauce or as-is!
notes: This wrap is best enjoyed fresh, though you could prep the lentils (step 2) in advance and store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 3 days.

title: Teriyaki Tofu & Veggie Wraps
description: Crunchy vegetables, teriyaki glazed tofu and a delicious peanut sauce make up this Asian flavours-inspired wrap. It comes together in only 20 minutes, so it's perfect for a busy weeknight, or when you don't really feel like cooking!

items: 6 Tbsp teriyaki sauce, plus more to taste
1 Tbsp sriracha, or less for less spicy
2 tsp sriracha, or omit for less heat
4 large soft flour tortillas (or gluten-free tortillas)

instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the tofu, cornstarch, and salt.
Heat the oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Cook the tofu for about 10 minutes, or until golden on all sides.
When the tofu is golden, add the teriyaki sauce and sriracha. Stir to coat, then remove from the heat.
In a small bowl, mix together the peanut sauce ingredients.
Spoon the peanut sauce over each wrap. Divide the veggies and prepared tofu between the wraps, then roll up.
Grill in a grill pan over medium heat for 1 to 2 minutes per side, until warmed through and lightly toasted, if desired. Enjoy!
notes: Traditionally, teriyaki sauce is made with just soy sauce, sake (or mirin), and sugar. Make sure to check the ingredients of your bottle so it doesn't have too many other ingredients that may affect the flavour. Our large tortillas measure about 10 inches (25 cm) in diameter. The assembled wrap is best when enjoyed immediately.

title: Red Lentil Curry
description: This one-pot Red Lentil Curry is a simple yet gourmet pantry recipe and bonafide fan favorite. Creamy, indulgent, and ready in 45 minutes.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon refined or virgin coconut oil, or a neutral-flavored oil
2 inch piece of fresh ginger, peeled and minced or grated
1 tablespoon minced fresh turmeric, or 1 teaspoon ground turmeric
2 chipotle peppers, diced, with some sauce
Kosher salt or sea salt to taste (about 1 tsp)
Freshly cracked black pepper to taste
1 cup (180-190g) red lentils, or split red lentils
3 tablespoons unsweetened creamy almond butter (or tahini)
For serving: Basmati or Jasmine rice and Indian flatbread such as roti, paratha, or naan
instructions: Rinse the lentils in cold water until the water runs clear.

Heat a large, deep skillet or large saucepan over medium-high heat and add the coconut oil. Once the oil is shimmering, add the garlic, ginger, fresh turmeric (if using), and Serrano pepper and cook for 2 minutes, stirring frequently to prevent garlic from burning.

Add the cumin, coriander, chili powder, curry powder, garam masala, salt, and black pepper to taste, and cook for 30 to 60 seconds until fragrant, tossing frequently to prevent burning. If using ground turmeric instead of fresh turmeric, add the ground turmeric now, along the the rest of the spices.

Pour in the vegetable broth, and use a wooden spoon or spatula to scrape up any browned bits on the bottom of the pot. Add in the lentils and the crushed tomatoes and mix well. Reduce the heat to low and cover the pan with a lid. Simmer for 20 to 25 minutes, or until the lentils are cooked through and have mostly softened.

Remove the lid and stir in the coconut milk, almond butter, along with salt and pepper to taste. Continue cooking on low heat, uncovered for 5 to 8 minutes, until the curry has thickened and is creamy.
Finally, stir in the lemon juice and cilantro, and turn off the heat. If you want your lentils to even creamier and more on the puréed side, use an immersion blender to lightly purée the curry.
Serve the curry with rice and/or Indian flatbread and garnish with additional cilantro. Store leftovers in the fridge for 3-4 days.
notes: If you can handle a little heat, use one Serrano pepper (remove the seeds) or one jalapeño pepper (with the seeds), and if you really like things on the spicy side, use two Serrano peppers (with seeds intact).
